stem: In \( \triangle ABC \), \( AB=[[a]] \), \( AC=[[b]] \), and the included angle \( A=\dfrac{\pi}{[[n]]} \). Find \( BC \) using the Law of Cosines.
solution: By the Law of Cosines, \( BC^{2}=AB^{2}+AC^{2}-2\cdot AB\cdot AC\cdot\cos A=[[a]]^{2}+[[b]]^{2}-2([[a]])([[b]])\cos\dfrac{\pi}{[[n]]} \), so \( BC=[[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This flips the sign in front of the cosine term, effectively using the exterior angle instead of the included angle.
This subtracts \( AC^{2}\) instead of adding it before subtracting the cosine term.
This reports \( BC^{2}\) itself instead of taking the square root.
This drops the required factor of \( 2\) in front of \( AB\cdot AC\cos A\).
This uses the sides and cosine additively instead of applying the Law of Cosines formula.
This is off by a small additive amount from the correct side length.

stem: A triangle has sides \( a=[[a]] \), \( b=[[b]] \), \( c=[[c]] \), where \( C \) is the angle opposite side \( c \). Find \( \cos C \).
solution: By the Law of Cosines, \( c^{2}=a^{2}+b^{2}-2ab\cos C \), so \( \cos C=\dfrac{a^{2}+b^{2}-c^{2}}{2ab}=[[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This adds \( c^{2}\) instead of subtracting it in the numerator.
This swaps the sign on \( b^{2}\) instead of \( c^{2}\).
This drops the required factor of \( 2\) in the denominator.
This negates the whole numerator, as if solving for the angle opposite a different side.
This is off by one from the correct value of \( \cos C\).
This is off by one (other direction) from the correct value of \( \cos C\).

stem: In \( \triangle ABC \), \( a=[[a]] \), \( A=\dfrac{\pi}{[[nA]]} \), and \( B=\dfrac{\pi}{[[nB]]} \). Find side \( b \) using the Law of Sines.
solution: By the Law of Sines, \( \dfrac{a}{\sin A}=\dfrac{b}{\sin B} \), so \( b=\dfrac{a\sin B}{\sin A}=\dfrac{[[a]]\sin\left(\pi/[[nB]]\right)}{\sin\left(\pi/[[nA]]\right)}=[[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This inverts the sine ratio, using \( \sin A/\sin B\) instead of \( \sin B/\sin A\).
This uses cosines instead of sines in the ratio.
This multiplies the two sines instead of dividing them.
This drops the given side \( a\) entirely.
This is off by one from the correct side length.
This is off by one (other direction) from the correct side length.

stem: In \( \triangle ABC \), \( a=[[a]] \), \( b=[[b]] \), and \( A=\dfrac{\pi}{[[nA]]} \). Find \( \sin B \) using the Law of Sines.
solution: By the Law of Sines, \( \dfrac{\sin A}{a}=\dfrac{\sin B}{b} \), so \( \sin B=\dfrac{b\sin A}{a}=\dfrac{[[b]]\sin\left(\pi/[[nA]]\right)}{[[a]]}=[[answer]] \); since \( b<a \), this is a valid triangle with \( B \) acute.
distractor_rationales: This inverts the ratio, using side \( a\) in the numerator instead of \( b\).
This uses \( \cos A\) instead of \( \sin A\).
This inverts the whole expression, dividing \( b\) by \( a\sin A\) instead of multiplying.
This drops the given side \( a\) from the denominator entirely.
This is off by a small additive amount from the correct value of \( \sin B\).
This is off by a small additive amount (other direction) from the correct value of \( \sin B\).
